answer:
In Part 1 of *Bang-e-Dara*, Iqbal's lyrical "we" evokes an inclusive, territorially rooted Indian patriotism centered on Hindustan as a shared homeland blending Muslim, Hindu, Sikh, Persian, and other legacies.[^1][^2] The collective voice celebrates the subcontinent's composite cultural and spiritual history—from Chishti and Nanak to biblical prophets and Tatar migrations—as "my homeland, this land of mine," positioning the "we" within a syncretic national geography guarded by the Himalaya.[^1][^2]

By Part 3, this shifts to a pan-Islamic "we" defined by the global Muslim *ummah* (*millat*) and its sacred abodes (Delhi, Baghdad, Cordoba, Constantinople, Medina), unbound by any single nation-state like India.[^3][^4][^5] The voice now laments Muslim decline in *Shikwa* as champions of God's oneness ("It was we, and we alone"), spans "Chin to Arab lands," and urges youth to reclaim Arabian forebears' world-conquering spirit, with Hijaz/Medina as the true "homeland" and heart.[^4][^5][^6] India appears but subordinated; the ummah's story transcends it.[^3]

This reflects Iqbal's evolution from early (pre-WWI) Hindustani nationalism to post-WWI advocacy for Muslim political self-determination.[^4]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 26: "Hindustani Bachon Ka Qaumi Geet" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/)).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 25: "Himalaya" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/25-himalaya/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/25-himalaya/translation/)).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 2: "Bilad-e-Islamiya" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/)).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 23: "Tarana-e-Milli" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/23-tarana-e-milli/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/23-tarana-e-milli/translation/)).

[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 20: "Shikwa" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/)).

[^6]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 13: "Khitab ba Jawanan-e-Islam" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/)).
